children.heading: Certification required for exemption of undercover operations from certain laws
children.label: a Certification required for exemption of undercover operations from certain laws
i
children.text: sections 1341 and 3324 of title 31,

children.label: ii
children.text: sections 6301(a) and (b)(1) to (3) and 6306 of title 41,

children.label: iii
children.text: chapter 45 of title 41,

children.label: iv
children.text: , andsection 8141 of title 40

children.label: v
children.text: , andsection 3901 of title 41

children.label: B
children.text: to establish or to acquire proprietary corporations or business entities as part of the undercover operation, and to operate such corporations or business entities on a commercial basis, without regard to sections 9102 and 9103 of title 31;

sums authorized to be appropriated for the Service and the proceeds from the undercover operation, may be deposited in banks or other financial institutions without regard to the provisions of and ; andsection 648 of title 18section 3302 of title 31
the proceeds from the undercover operation may be used to offset necessary and reasonable expenses incurred in such operation without regard to the provisions of ;section 3302 of title 31

children.heading: Liquidation of corporations and business entities
children.label: b Liquidation of corporations and business entities
children.text: If a corporation or business entity established or acquired as part of an undercover operation under paragraph (1)(B) of subsection (a) with a net value over $50,000 is to be liquidated, sold, or otherwise disposed of, the Service, as much in advance as the Commissioner or his designee determines is practicable, shall report the circumstances to the Secretary of the Treasury. The proceeds of the liquidation, sale, or other disposition, after obligations are met, shall be deposited in the Treasury of the United States as miscellaneous receipts.

children.heading: Deposit of proceeds
children.label: c Deposit of proceeds
children.text: As soon as the proceeds from an undercover investigative operation with respect to which an action is authorized and carried out under paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (a) of this section are no longer necessary for the conduct of such operation, such proceeds or the balance of such proceeds remaining at the time shall be deposited into the Treasury of the United States as miscellaneous receipts.

children.label: A
children.text: submit the results of the audit in writing to the Secretary of the Treasury; and

children.label: B
children.text: not later than 180 days after such undercover operation is closed, submit a report to the Congress concerning such audit.

children.label: A
children.text: the number, by programs, of undercover investigative operations pending as of the end of the 1-year period for which such report is submitted;

children.label: B
children.text: the number, by programs, of undercover investigative operations commenced in the 1-year period preceding the period for which such report is submitted; and

children.label: C
children.text: the number, by programs, of undercover investigative operations closed in the 1-year period preceding the period for which such report is submitted and, with respect to each such closed undercover operation, the results obtained and any civil claims made with respect thereto.

children.label: A
children.text: all criminal proceedings (other than appeals) are concluded, or

children.label: B
children.text: covert activities are concluded, whichever occurs later.
The term “employees” means employees, as defined in , of the Service.section 2105 of title 5

children.label: i
children.text: the gross receipts (excluding interest earned) exceed $50,000, or

children.label: ii
children.text: expenditures (other than expenditures for salaries of employees) exceed $150,000; and

children.label: B
children.text: which is exempt from section 3302 or 9102 of title 31;
